message:
fix(time): synchronize timer wheel to current clock before register/update/query paths

The timer wheel's internal current_tick only advanced during
collect_expired() calls.  When the system was idle for extended
periods (e.g. 8+ days of virtual time), subsequent register() or
next_deadline() calls would measure new deadlines against a stale
baseline, causing short future timers to be classified as already
expired and fire immediately.

Fix (wheel.rs): New crate-visible synchronize(now) method advances
buckets and overflow promotion to the provided time without draining
ready timers.  collect_expired() now delegates to synchronize()
instead of duplicating the advance logic.

Fix (driver.rs): register(), update(), and next_deadline() now call
wheel.synchronize(now) under the wheel lock before performing their
operations, ensuring the wheel's time baseline is current.  The
clock sample is taken after acquiring the wheel lock to prevent
stale-time from lock contention.

Tests:
- timer_driver_register_after_idle_gap_uses_current_clock_baseline:
  advances clock by 8 days, registers a 1-second timer, verifies
  it fires at the correct future time (not immediately).
- timer_driver_update_after_idle_gap_keeps_future_deadline: updates
  a timer after an 8-day idle gap, verifies the updated deadline
  is respected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
